title: Terraform S3 and CloudFront module for React frontend
description: Terraform module for React frontend hosting. Private S3 bucket with versioning and CloudFront distribution using Origin Access Control (OAC). HTTPS enforced, custom error page for SPA routing.

acceptance_criteria.then: public access is blocked on all 4 settings and the bucket is not publicly accessible
it uses Origin Access Control (OAC) not Origin Access Identity (OAI)

acceptance_criteria.when: accessing the CloudFront URL over HTTP
acceptance_criteria.then: it redirects to HTTPS with a 301 response

acceptance_criteria.when: accessing a non-existent path on the CloudFront URL
acceptance_criteria.then: it returns index.html with a 200 status code for SPA routing

non_functional_requirements.security: S3 bucket must block all public access. CloudFront must use OAC. TLS 1.2 minimum. WAF optional for prod.
non_functional_requirements.performance: CloudFront cache TTL: 1 year for hashed assets, no-cache for index.html.

definition_of_done: S3 bucket private with versioning enabled
Custom error page routes all 404s to index.html
agent_instructions: Read .claude/constitutions/terraform.md before writing any .tf files. Use OAC not OAI — OAI is deprecated. The S3 bucket must have zero public access. The CloudFront error page must return 200 not 404 for /index.html to support React Router.
